# Generated by ollama (codellama)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: web-server
  type: compute.v1.instance
  properties:
    zone: us-central1-a
    machineType: f1-micro
    disks:
    - deviceName: boot
      autoDelete: true
      initializeParams:
        sourceImage: projects/debian-cloud/global/images/family/debian-9
    networkInterfaces:
    - network: global/networks/default
      accessConfigs:
      - name: External NAT
        type: ONE_TO_ONE_NAT
    metadata:
      startup-script: |-
        #!/bin/bash
        sudo apt-get update
        sudo apt-get install nginx -y
        sudo service nginx start
  dependsOn: [cloud-sql-instance]
- name: cloud-sql-instance
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    instanceType: SQL_INSTANCE_TYPE_BY_NAME
    settings:
      tier: D0
      dataDiskSizeGb: 10
      activationPolicy: ALWAYS
- name: cloud-sql-database
  type: sqladmin.v1beta4.instance
  properties:
    instanceType: SQL_INSTANCE_TYPE_BY_NAME
    settings:
      tier: D0
      dataDiskSizeGb: 10
      activationPolicy: ALWAYS
- name: cloud-storage-bucket
  type: storage.v1.bucket
  properties:
    location: US
    bucketPolicyOnly: true
    uniformBucketLevelAccess: true
    versioning:
      enabled: true
